100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On June 8, 1926, tensions escalated in the Far East as Japan and China continued to clash over territorial disputes, raising concerns among Western powers. In Europe, the League of Nations grappled with economic instability, as member nations debated measures to address the ongoing repercussions of the Great War. Meanwhile, in the United States, the aviation industry celebrated a significant milestone with Charles Lindbergh's historic transatlantic flight still inspiring innovation and interest in air travel.
Updated: Monday, June 8, 2026 at 10:18:28 AM CDT
